description: Improve customer onboarding and payment flow: auto-detect payment after Stripe, show pricing, first-use guide, better signup page, and forgot password.

userStories.title: Auto-detect payment after Stripe return
userStories.description: As a customer returning from Stripe checkout, I want the app to detect my payment automatically so I don't get stuck on the paywall.
acceptanceCriteria: In App.tsx: on mount, check URL for ?billing=success param
When ?billing=success is present, start polling GET /api/billing/status every 2 seconds (max 10 attempts)
When payment_active returns true during polling, update paymentActive state to true (paywall disappears)
If after 10 attempts payment_active is still false, show message 'Pagamento em processamento. Atualize a pagina em alguns segundos.' on the paywall screen
After processing, remove ?billing=success from URL using window.history.replaceState (no page reload)
When ?billing=cancel is in URL, show message 'Assinatura cancelada' on the paywall screen and remove the param
Polling cleanup: clear interval on unmount or when payment detected
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: In frontend/src/App.tsx: Add a useEffect that checks window.location.search for billing=success on mount. Use setInterval for polling. The existing billing status fetch (line ~25-38) already calls GET /api/billing/status — reuse the same pattern. When polling detects payment_active=true, call setPaymentActive(true) which will cause the paywall conditional to pass. Use window.history.replaceState(null, '', window.location.pathname) to clean the URL. For billing=cancel, just show a one-time message and clean URL.

userStories.title: Show pricing on paywall and billing page
userStories.description: As a customer on the paywall screen, I want to see the subscription price so I know how much I'll pay before clicking subscribe.
acceptanceCriteria: In App.tsx paywall screen: add text 'Plano mensal — R$ 349,90/mes' between the warning box and the BillingPage component
Style: fontSize var(--text-sm), color var(--ink-muted), fontWeight 600, centered
In BillingPage.tsx: when payment_active is false, show 'Plano mensal — R$ 349,90/mes' above the 'Assinar' button
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
In frontend/src/App.tsx: the paywall block is the section starting with 'if (billingAvailable && !paymentActive && !auth.user?.is_super_admin)'. Add a

element with the pricing text right before the component. In frontend/src/pages/BillingPage.tsx: add the same pricing text inside the !payment_active branch, above the 'Assinar' button (around line 147).

userStories.title: First-use onboarding guide
userStories.description: As a new customer who just paid, I want to see a quick guide so I know how to start using the system.
acceptanceCriteria: In App.tsx: after the header and before the content area, show a welcome card if localStorage key 'onboarding-done' is not set
The card has title 'Bem-vindo ao Copy Anuncios!' and 3 numbered steps:
Step 1: 'Conecte sua conta do Mercado Livre na aba Admin > Sellers'
Step 2: 'Cole os IDs dos anuncios que deseja copiar'
Step 3: 'Selecione origem e destino e clique Copiar'
The card has a button 'Entendi' that sets localStorage 'onboarding-done' to 'true' and hides the card
Card uses same design system: Card-like styling with var(--surface) background, rounded corners, padding
Only shows for non-super-admin users
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: In frontend/src/App.tsx: add a state `const [showOnboarding, setShowOnboarding] = useState(() => !localStorage.getItem('onboarding-done'))`. Render the card between the header and the {/* Content */} section, only when showOnboarding && !auth.user?.is_super_admin. On 'Entendi' click: localStorage.setItem('onboarding-done', 'true') and setShowOnboarding(false). Use inline styles matching the existing design system (var(--surface), var(--space-*), var(--text-*), rounded corners).

userStories.title: Improve signup page with product info and pricing
userStories.description: As a visitor on the signup page, I want to understand what the product does so I feel confident creating an account.
acceptanceCriteria: In Signup.tsx: below the existing subtitle 'Crie sua conta para comecar', add a new subtitle: 'Copie anuncios e compatibilidades entre contas do Mercado Livre'
Below that subtitle, add 3 bullet points with checkmark prefix: 'Copia em massa entre contas', 'Compatibilidades veiculares automaticas', 'Gerenciamento multi-conta'
Below the form (before the 'Ja tem conta?' link), add text: 'Plano mensal: R$ 349,90/mes'
Bullet points use fontSize var(--text-xs), color var(--ink-muted), left-aligned within the card
Pricing text uses fontSize var(--text-xs), color var(--ink-faint), centered
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
In frontend/src/pages/Signup.tsx: The subtitle is at ~line 74-81. Add the product description below it (before the
). For bullet points, use a simple div with 3

elements, each with a checkmark character. For pricing, add a

between the

and the 'Ja tem conta?' button (~line 139). Keep the same styling patterns used in the file.

userStories.title: Forgot password — database migration
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a password_reset_tokens table so that reset tokens can be stored and validated.
acceptanceCriteria: Create migration file app/db/migrations/009_password_reset_tokens.sql

Table password_reset_tokens with columns: id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE, token TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL, expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
Create index idx_password_reset_tokens_token ON password_reset_tokens(token)
Create index idx_password_reset_tokens_user ON password_reset_tokens(user_id)
Use IF NOT EXISTS for CREATE TABLE and CREATE INDEX
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Follow the existing migration pattern in app/db/migrations/ (files 001-008 exist). Use IF NOT EXISTS for idempotency. The user_id FK references users(id) with ON DELETE CASCADE so tokens are cleaned up when a user is deleted.

userStories.title: Forgot password — backend endpoints
userStories.description: As a developer, I want forgot-password and reset-password endpoints so that customers can recover their accounts.
acceptanceCriteria: Add POST /api/auth/forgot-password endpoint in app/routers/auth.py
ForgotPasswordRequest model with field: email (str)
Endpoint looks up user by email. If found, generates token via secrets.token_urlsafe(32), inserts into password_reset_tokens with expires_at = now + 1 hour
Always returns 200 with {message: 'Se o email existir, enviaremos instrucoes'} regardless of whether user exists (security)
Add POST /api/auth/reset-password endpoint in app/routers/auth.py
ResetPasswordRequest model with fields: token (str), new_password (str)
Validates new_password >= 6 chars, returns 400 if too short
Looks up token in password_reset_tokens, checks not expired, returns 400 'Link expirado ou invalido' if invalid
Updates user password_hash using existing _hash_password(), deletes the token row, returns 200 {message: 'Senha alterada com sucesso'}
Both endpoints are public (no auth required)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: In app/routers/auth.py: add two new endpoints after the existing /logout endpoint. Use the same patterns: Pydantic BaseModel for request, get_db() for queries, _hash_password() for hashing. The forgot-password endpoint must NOT reveal whether the email exists. For token lookup: db.table('password_reset_tokens').select('*').eq('token', req.token).execute(). Check expires_at > now(utc). After successful reset, delete token: db.table('password_reset_tokens').delete().eq('id', token_row['id']).execute(). No email sending in this story — that's US-008.

userStories.title: Forgot password — frontend pages
userStories.description: As a customer who forgot their password, I want a flow in the login screen to reset it.
acceptanceCriteria: In Login.tsx: add link 'Esqueci minha senha' below the submit button, styled like the 'Ainda nao tem conta?' link
Clicking it calls an onNavigateToForgotPassword callback prop (optional, same pattern as onNavigateToSignup)
Create frontend/src/pages/ForgotPassword.tsx: same card layout as Login.tsx, with email input and 'Enviar link' button
On submit, POST /api/auth/forgot-password. Always show message 'Se o email existir, enviaremos instrucoes de redefinicao.' with a 'Voltar ao login' link
Create frontend/src/pages/ResetPassword.tsx: same card layout, with 'Nova senha' and 'Confirmar senha' inputs and 'Redefinir' button
Validates passwords match before submit. If mismatch, show 'Senhas nao conferem'
On submit, POST /api/auth/reset-password with {token, new_password}. On success show 'Senha alterada com sucesso' with 'Ir para login' button. On error show 'Link expirado. Solicite um novo.'
In App.tsx: add authView states 'forgot' and 'reset'. Check URL for ?reset_token=xxx on mount — if present, show ResetPassword with that token
Wire all navigation callbacks in App.tsx (login->forgot, forgot->login, reset->login)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: In frontend/src/App.tsx: extend AuthView type to 'login' | 'signup' | 'forgot' | 'reset'. Add useEffect to check for ?reset_token= in URL on mount. The ForgotPassword and ResetPassword pages follow the exact same pattern as Login.tsx and Signup.tsx: same card, same inputStyle, same button styles. ForgotPassword props: {onSubmit: (email) => Promise<void>, onNavigateToLogin: () => void}. ResetPassword props: {token: string, onNavigateToLogin: () => void}. In ResetPassword, the component handles the API call internally since it needs to show success/error states.

userStories.title: Send password reset email via SMTP
userStories.description: As the system, I want to send an email with the reset link so the customer can actually reset their password.
acceptanceCriteria: Add SMTP config to app/config.py: smtp_host: str = '', smtp_port: int = 587, smtp_user: str = '', smtp_password: str = '', smtp_from: str = ''
Create app/services/email.py with function send_reset_email(to_email: str, reset_token: str)
The function builds the reset link: f'{settings.base_url}?reset_token={reset_token}'
Email subject: 'Copy Anuncios — Redefinir senha'

Email body (plain text): 'Voce solicitou a redefinicao de senha.

Clique no link abaixo:
{link}

Este link expira em 1 hora.

Se voce nao solicitou, ignore este email.'
Uses Python smtplib with STARTTLS for sending
If smtp_host is empty, log warning 'Email not configured, skipping reset email' and return without error
In app/routers/auth.py forgot-password endpoint: after creating the token, call send_reset_email(). Wrap in try/except — if email fails, log error but still return 200
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Create new file app/services/email.py. Use smtplib.SMTP(settings.smtp_host, settings.smtp_port) with starttls() and login(). Build email with email.mime.text.MIMEText. In the forgot-password endpoint in auth.py, import and call send_reset_email after inserting the token. The call must be in a try/except so email failures don't break the endpoint. Check settings.smtp_host before attempting to send.
